title: Refreshing Raw-friendly Korean Lamb Cold Noodles
description: A light and refreshing Korean cold noodle dish featuring lamb shoulder, perfect for hot summer days.
instructions: In a large pot, bring 4 cups of water to a boil and cook the lamb shoulder until tender, about 2 hours.
Let the lamb cool completely, then shred or chop it into bite-sized pieces.
In a large bowl, whisk together soy sauce, rice vinegar, honey, sesame oil, and Korean chili flakes to make the dressing.
Add the cooked noodles to the bowl and toss with the dressing until the noodles are well coated.
Add the chopped lamb, grated ginger, and chopped green onions to the bowl and toss to combine.
Thinly slice the cucumber and add it to the bowl.
Chill the mixture in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes before serving.
Serve the Refreshing Raw-friendly Korean Lamb Cold Noodles chilled, garnished with additional green onions if desired.
